_comment_remote: GoalOps MCP — Claude Desktop config (Windows + macOS).
Claude Desktop's mcpServers schema only supports stdio MCP servers
(command + args). Direct `type: http` configs work in Claude Code but
are rejected by Claude Desktop as 'invalid configuration'. So we bridge
the remote HTTP MCP through `mcp-remote` (Anthropic's stdio<->HTTP
adapter, pulled via npx on first run).
Requirements:
- Node 20+ on the user's machine (winget install OpenJS.NodeJS.LTS)
- Network access to https://goal.996007.fun/mcp (basic_auth credentials
from your admin)
Windows note: Claude Desktop on Windows spawns the `command` directly,
without going through cmd.exe, so it can't resolve npx.cmd by name. We
wrap with `cmd /c npx ...` to make this reliable. macOS users can drop
the cmd /c wrapper and use `"command": "npx"` directly.
Replace REPLACE_ME_BASE64 with `base64(username:password)`.
Windows  : [Convert]::ToBase64String([Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes('goalops:THE_PASSWORD'))
macOS    : printf 'goalops:THE_PASSWORD' | base64
Config file location:
Windows : %APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json
macOS   : ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json
After saving, fully quit Claude Desktop (system tray -> Quit on Windows,
menu bar -> Quit on macOS) and reopen. The first connection takes a few
seconds while npx downloads mcp-remote.
